scope: Unsloth FastLanguageModel integration for ALM 14B LoRA — r7 stack (NOT r6)
stacks_on: shared/state/alm14b_pt_speedup_audit_20260415.json — base 7 speedup patches (FA2, compile, fused AdamW, disable_act_ckpt, dataloader, NCCL, TF32)

qwen25_compat.layer_support_gaps: None observed for Qwen2 family. Unsloth ships Qwen2.5 LoRA Colab notebook and a pre-patched model mirror. Architecture matches Qwen/Qwen2.5-14B-Instruct (14.7B params, 48 layers, 40 Q heads / 8 KV heads GQA, RoPE/SwiGLU/RMSNorm).

named_args_used_in_patch: load_in_4bit=False (REQUIRED — default True violates no-quantization rule)
named_args_NOT_in_signature: attn_implementation (Unsloth binds FA internally; passing via **kwargs is no-op or conflict)
device_map (Unsloth defaults to 'sequential'; passing 'auto' from HF path is incompatible)

fsdp_conflict.official_quote: Unsloth currently supports multi-GPU setups through libraries like Accelerate and DeepSpeed. This means you can already leverage parallelism methods such as FSDP and DDP with Unsloth. However, the process can be complex and requires manual setup. We're working hard to make multi-GPU support much simpler and more user-friendly.
fsdp_conflict.open_issues_referenced: github.com/unslothai/unsloth issues #3551 (GRPO+FSDP2 bug, on roadmap), #4082 (V100 BF16 full-ft bug), #3622 (Llama-4 loading), #3775/#4133 (closed)
fsdp_conflict.verdict: NOT_SAFE for in-script FSDP wrap on r7. Unsloth's internal torch.compile(fullgraph=True) and gradient checkpointing variant collide with the existing FSDP path (transformer_auto_wrap_policy + FULL_SHARD). Patch therefore ABORTS if --unsloth AND (--fsdp OR --ddp) are both set.
fsdp_conflict.single_gpu_impact: Zero — r5/r6 on single H100 does not use FSDP (uses device_map='auto' which Unsloth replaces).

fa2_conflict.unsloth_behavior: binds its own flash-attention path internally (HAS_FLASH_ATTENTION probe in loader.py) — does NOT accept HF's attn_implementation kwarg as a named arg
fa2_conflict.base_patch_interaction: alm14b_speedup_flash_attention.diff adds attn_implementation='flash_attention_2' to AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained calls. On the --unsloth path, this call is REPLACED by FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained which has its own FA binding. No conflict at runtime because the two code paths are mutually exclusive.
fa2_conflict.verdict: REPLACED — Unsloth's FA supersedes FA2 patch on the unsloth branch; FA2 patch still active on the non-unsloth branch.

compile_conflict.unsloth_behavior: Unsloth calls unsloth_compile_transformers() internally with fullgraph=True (per loader.py imports). User-level torch.compile(mode='max-autotune') on top would double-compile, break Dynamo traces, and can hang.
compile_conflict.base_patch_interaction: alm14b_speedup_torch_compile.diff wraps model with torch.compile(mode='max-autotune', dynamic=False, fullgraph=False) AFTER get_peft_model. On --unsloth, this wrap is SKIPPED (the if-guard now reads `if args.torch_compile and not args.unsloth`).
compile_conflict.verdict: REPLACED — Unsloth compiles internally; user compile patch is bypassed on unsloth branch.

grad_ckpt_conflict.unsloth_behavior: Unsloth's use_gradient_checkpointing='unsloth' is a custom (non-reentrant) variant that owns the checkpointing hooks. Calling HF model.gradient_checkpointing_enable() on top corrupts Unsloth's hooks.
grad_ckpt_conflict.base_patch_interaction: alm14b_speedup_disable_act_ckpt.diff is a LAUNCHER flag (not a trainer edit). The launcher passes --no-grad-ckpt. That patch is orthogonal to this one — it touches training/launch_alm_14b_r5.sh only.
grad_ckpt_conflict.verdict: REPLACED on trainer side — Unsloth handles ckpt via from_pretrained kwarg; launcher --no-grad-ckpt flag is orthogonal and still works.

liger_exclusivity.status: Liger Kernels not integrated in the current trainer. Unsloth and Liger are mutually exclusive approaches (both rewrite attention + RMSNorm + cross-entropy). Not a current conflict because Liger path does not exist in training/train_alm_14b.py.
liger_exclusivity.future_note: If/when a Liger patch lands (separate audit), --unsloth and --liger must be mutually exclusive flags.

expected_uplift_range.headline_vs_hf_default: 2.0x-2.4x faster + 50-74% less memory (cited, HF model card unsloth/Qwen2.5-14B-Instruct)

r6_to_r7_delta_estimate.confidence: qualitative — NOT benchmarked on our pod
r6_to_r7_delta_estimate.reasoning: r6 already stacks FA2 + fused AdamW + compile + no act-ckpt + async dataloader. Unsloth's 2x headline is vs bare HF+peft; our r6 already captures ~45-60% of that. The remaining delta comes from Unsloth's fused LoRA backward kernels and custom gradient checkpointing.

expected_uplift_range.validation_plan: empirical measurement on H100 pod AFTER r6 establishes a baseline at seq=1024 bf16 LoRA r=128. No uplift numbers fabricated — cite only HF model card.

r6_launch.reason: r6 is in flight; do not disrupt. Establishes baseline for r7 A/B.

r7_launch.torch_compile_behavior: if --torch-compile is also passed, it is IGNORED with a WARNING (Unsloth compiles internally)
r7_launch.reason: Stack claims 2x-2.4x headline uplift + 50-74% VRAM reduction vs HF+peft per HF model card; realistic delta over r6 is +15-35% throughput.

r7_multigpu_variant.reason: Unsloth multi-GPU requires manual Accelerate/DeepSpeed setup (out of scope). For 2xH100 14B and 32B r1, stay on base-7.

blockers.description: pod must have `pip install unsloth` before r7 launch. Audit policy forbids installing on the H100 pod during audit phase, so this remains as a manual ops step at r7 launch time.
blockers.mitigation: lazy import inside `if args.unsloth` so the module is only imported when the flag is set — r6 launch is unaffected even if unsloth is not installed.

blockers.description: FastLanguageModel.from_pretrained returns (model, tokenizer) tuple. The patch re-binds the `tokenizer` local variable to Unsloth's copy and re-checks pad_token — this is necessary because Unsloth applies fix_tokenizer=True by default.
the +15-35% r6→r7 delta is a qualitative estimate. Real measurement requires running r6 to get a baseline step/min rate, then r7 smoke test.
blockers.mitigation: validation_plan above — measure at step 50 of r7 smoke with `nvidia-smi --query-gpu=memory.used` and wall-clock step rate.

git_apply_check: PASS (verified against working tree with base-7 patches already applied — 499-line r6 state)
composed_python_parse: PASS (ast.parse on r6+unsloth composition yields valid AST, 3088 nodes)

conflicts_with_base7.flash_attention: REPLACED on unsloth branch, preserved on non-unsloth branch
conflicts_with_base7.torch_compile: REPLACED on unsloth branch, preserved on non-unsloth branch
conflicts_with_base7.nccl_env: KEPT — launcher env is unchanged (no-op for single-GPU anyway)
conflicts_with_base7.tf32_cudnn: KEPT — global torch backend toggle, compatible

feature_flag_design.rationale: feature flag makes r6 (base-7) launch immune to this patch — only r7 launch opts in by passing --unsloth. Imports are lazy inside the branch so unsloth does NOT need to be installed for r6.
feature_flag_design.mutual_exclusion: runtime RuntimeError if --unsloth AND (--fsdp OR --ddp); runtime WARNING if --unsloth AND --torch-compile (compile ignored on unsloth path)

sign_off.rule_never_resume_with_param_change: honored — Unsloth path only activates on fresh r7 launch
sign_off.rule_one_shot_best: honored — patch prepared BEFORE r7 launch; r6 baseline feeds r7 uplift measurement
sign_off.rule_do_not_disrupt_r6: honored — r6 cmdline does NOT pass --unsloth; patch is dormant on r6 path
sign_off.rule_do_not_install_unsloth_on_pod: honored — lazy import, audit-only patch stage
sign_off.rule_hexa_first: N/A — training is .py (pre-existing exemption per training/CLAUDE.md)

apply_to: r7 launch (fresh, step 0, after r6 baseline established)
